Our Story

Where Faith Meets Creativity

King Kaps Creative Hub is a visual arts enterprise rooted in Embul-bul, Ngong, at the doorstep of Kajiado County's rich Maasai heritage. We create original, handcrafted artwork using recycled and everyday materials — transforming what others discard into pieces that command attention in homes, hotels, offices, and galleries.


Every piece we sell carries a tithe — a portion given directly to the church and community. Art is our calling. Giving is our culture. Quality is our promise.


Founded by Peter Maina (Owner & Creative Director) and Francis Mwangi (Managing Director), we serve clients across Ngong, Karen, Langata, and beyond.

The People Behind the Art

Meet Our Team

Real faces, real people — this is who you are buying from. We stand behind every piece we create.

Founder · Creative Director

The visionary behind King Kaps. Peter transforms everyday recycled materials into stunning works of art, blending Maasai heritage with modern design. Based in Ngong, he leads all creative direction and brings each commission to life with faith and passion.

Our Collection

Six Signature Lines

Request Custom Piece
Spoon & Straw Series
Gold Straw Flowers
Drinking straws and plastic spoons transformed into stunning gold wall pieces. Perfect for living rooms, salons, and offices.
Ksh 4,000 – 15,000
Wall Art
Spoon Wreath Sculptures
Layered plastic spoons painted gold, arranged into wreaths and sunbursts that look like premium metalwork.
Ksh 5,000 – 18,000
Mixed Media
Paper & Recycled Art
Newspaper, cardboard, and everyday waste transformed into compelling statement pieces for churches, schools, and events.
Ksh 2,500 – 8,000
Spoon Art
Spoon Mirror Frames
Mirrors framed with carefully arranged painted spoons — stunning statement pieces loved by interior designers and AirBnBs.
Ksh 6,000 – 18,000
Custom Commission
Bespoke Art Pieces
Have a unique vision? We build it. From sports themes to corporate art — any material, any size, any message.
Ksh 15,000 +
Maasai Heritage
Cultural Series
Authentic Kajiado patterns and Maasai colours woven into collectible art. Prized by tourists, hotels, and the diaspora.
Ksh 8,000 – 25,000
✦ Our Secret Weapon

The Maasai
Heritage Series

Based in the heart of Kajiado — the homeland of the Maasai — we authentically incorporate traditional patterns, beadwork colours, and cultural symbols into our art using recycled materials. This gives King Kaps a story that international buyers, tourists, and the diaspora are willing to pay a premium for.

Premium Pricing International Buyers Culturally Authentic Tourist Market
